events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need reason. Scenario: commercial construction, fall protection, anchor point inadequate (guessing at 5,000-lb rating - TRD). Work in progress. Need identify hazards severity immediate controls. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Anchor point inadequate for fall arrest could lead to death. Severity critical. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 100? The anchor is installed but defective/non-compliant. Work_stage in-progress. Findings: anchor point not rated/verified for 5,000 lb per worker or not designed per qualified person; relying on guess/TRD; no documentation/calculations/certification; potential for anchor failure during fall arrest. Actions: immediately stop use of anchor; remove workers from fall hazard; have qualified person evaluate/design anchor; install certified anchor with 5,000 lb capacity per attached worker or maintain safety factor of at least two under 1926.502(d)(15); provide documentation/submittal; inspect/test; train workers; ensure PFAS use. References: OSHA 1926.502(d)(15), 1926.500, 1910.140(c)(13). Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 100,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "findings": [
    "Anchor point is being used without a verified 5,000-lb capacity per attached worker.",
    "Anchor rating is based on guess/TRD rather than engineering evaluation, manufacturer data, or qualified-person design.",
    "Inadequate anchor creates risk of failure during fall arrest, exposing workers to serious injury or death.",
    "No documented certification or submittal confirming the anchor meets OSHA fall-arrest anchor requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately prohibit use of the suspect anchor and remove workers from the fall hazard until a compliant anchor is installed.",
    "Have a qualified person evaluate, design, or select an anchor capable of 5,000 lb per attached worker, or meet the safety-factor requirements of the standard.",
    "Install a certified anchor with proper documentation, submittal, and inspection.",
    "Inspect and test the anchor system before returning workers to the area.",
    "Retrain workers on proper anchor selection, use of PFAS, and hazard recognition."
  ],
  "rationale": "An unverified anchor point cannot support fall-arrest loads. OSHA requires each anchor to be capable of supporting at least 5,000 lb per attached worker or be designed, installed, and used under the supervision of a qualified person as part of a complete personal fall-arrest system that maintains a safety factor of at least two. Guessing the rating is unacceptable and creates an imminent danger of anchor failure.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M",
    "OSHA 1926.502(d)(15)",
    "OSHA 1910.140(c)(13)"
  ]
}
